name: Mile-High Chocolate Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine cocoa, hot water, and baking soda in a small bowl. Let stand while mixing other ingredients.
2. Cream shortening and sugar together in a mixing bowl using an electric mixer set on medium speed until light and fluffy.
3. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition. Beat in vanilla and cocoa mixture.
4. Alternately add flour with buttermilk to the creamed mixture, beating well after each addition.
5. Pour batter into 3 greased and wax paper lined 8-inch round cake pans.
6. Bake in a preheated 350 degree F oven for 25 minutes or until cakes are done when tested.
7. Cool in pans on racks for 10 minutes. Remove from pans; cool completely on racks.
8. Assemble the cake by placing one cake layer on a serving plate. Spread with Cocoa Frosting.
9. Top with second cake layer and spread a layer of frosting.
10. Top with third cake layer and frost sides and top with remaining frosting.
Cocoa Frosting:
1. Combine butter and baking chocolate in the top of a double boiler. Place over hot water; stir until melted. Remove from heat; cool to room temperature.
2. Sift confectioners' sugar into a large mixing bowl. Make a well in the center and add egg white, vanilla, and lemon juice.
3. Pour in the chocolate mixture. Blend until smooth using an electric mixer set at medium speed.
4. Add milk to make frosting of a spreading consistency.
